sentences.base_language: I love going to the market on Sunday mornings.
sentences.target_language: J'adore aller au marché le dimanche matin.

sentences.base_language: The market is full of fresh produce.
sentences.target_language: Le marché est plein de produits frais.

sentences.base_language: Good morning! How much are the tomatoes?

sentences.target_language: J'en prends deux kilos, s'il vous plaît.
sentences.grammarNote: 'En' replaces a quantity or a partitive: 'J'en prends deux kilos' instead of 'Je prends deux kilos de tomates'. En is a very common pronoun in French.

sentences.base_language: Do you have any strawberries today?
Yes, they're from the region. Very sweet.
sentences.target_language: Oui, elles viennent de la région. Très sucrées.

sentences.base_language: That makes seven euros in total.
sentences.target_language: Ça fait sept euros en tout.

sentences.base_language: Here you go. Have a good day!
